name: Mint Payday Loans
description_short: Mint Payday Loans offers short-term payday loans up to $1,000 in Denver with same-day or next-day funding for all credit types, including those with poor credit scores.
description_long:
Mint Payday Loans operates as an online payday lending service in Denver, Colorado, facilitating short-term borrowing for residents facing urgent cash needs. The company positions itself as a fast-access solution for covering surprise expenses and financial emergencies without requiring collateral or extensive credit checks. Their service model emphasizes speed and accessibility, claiming approval and funding can occur within hours or by the next business day.

The company offers three primary loan products: standard Payday Loans up to $1,000, Cash Advance Loans up to $1,000, and Bad Credit Loans ranging from $250 to $1,000. All products are marketed as unsecured loans welcoming applicants of all credit types. Their stated process consists of three steps: submitting an online form, receiving approval within hours after electronic review, and receiving funds deposited into a bank account same-day or next business day. They advertise 256-bit SSL and TLS security for online applications.

Mint Payday Loans distinguishes itself through customer testimonials highlighting transparency, reasonable terms relative to credit profiles, and willingness to work with applicants lacking traditional bank accounts. They provide explicit fee disclosures (ranging $10-$30 per $100 borrowed, with $15 per $100 as typical) and example calculations to help borrowers understand total repayment amounts upfront. The company emphasizes education about payday loan mechanics and Colorado's regulatory framework, including the state's $500 maximum borrowing limit without collateral.

However, payday loans inherently carry significant drawbacks despite Mint's transparency efforts. The high fees compound to extremely high annualized APRs (a $15 fee per $100 represents approximately 391% APR on a 2-week loan). The short repayment timeline and debt rollover potential create risk of repeated borrowing cycles. While customer reviews are positive, they are self-selected testimonials displayed on the company's own website, limiting independent verification of satisfaction levels. Colorado residents should view payday loans as genuine emergency-only options, not sustainable financial solutions.
pros: Same-day or next-day funding claimed upon approval, addressing urgent cash needs
Loan amounts up to $1,000 available, larger than typical payday loan minimums
Explicit fee transparency with specific examples ($15 per $100 is 'common' fee, total repayment example provided)
Willingness to work with applicants lacking bank accounts, addressing underbanked populations
All credit types welcome, including poor/bad credit borrowers typically rejected elsewhere
Entirely online application process with no in-person visit required
Colorado-compliant lending with acknowledgment of state's $500 no-collateral maximum
cons: Fees of $10-$30 per $100 borrowed translate to APRs of 260%-780% annualized, far exceeding payday-alternatives category rates
Short repayment timeline (typically within weeks) creates high risk of debt rollover and repeated borrowing cycles
Customer reviews are exclusively company-displayed testimonials with no independent verification or third-party ratings visible
Requires proof of regular income and valid government ID, excluding gig workers with irregular income documentation
Website lacks disclosure of actual APR or default consequences, despite mentioning fee structures
best_for: Employed individuals with poor credit facing immediate emergency expenses (medical, utilities, rent) with clear next-paycheck repayment capacity
Unbanked or underbanked Denver residents needing emergency cash access without traditional banking relationships
Borrowers explicitly comparing payday loans as a last resort after exhausting credit union PALs and employer advance options
diagnosis: Mint Payday Loans is appropriate only for employed Denver residents facing genuine emergencies who cannot access payday-alternative products (credit union PALs, employer advances, CDFIs) and fully understand the 260%-780% annualized cost. The critical caveat is that payday loans should never be a recurring borrowing solution—the fee structure and short terms create debt spirals that worsen financial stability despite the company's transparency efforts.
services: Payday loans up to $1,000 for all credit types
Cash advance loans up to $1,000 for short-term cash needs
Bad credit loans from $250-$1,000 specifically marketed to poor credit applicants
Same-day loan approval following application review and verification
Online application submission with multi-step approval process
Electronic loan document signing and contract review
Same-day or next-business-day direct deposit of funds to applicant bank account
Loan amount calculator tool ('Express Loan - Get a Free Quote')
Fee estimation and total repayment amount calculation for transparency
Colorado-specific loan terms and regulatory compliance documentation
